articles.title: Relief for residents as dust storm lashes Delhi NCR amid scorching heatwave, rain expected - India.Com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Delhi
articles.full_text:
Delhi weather update: Residents of Delhi-NCR, who had been struggling with intense heat and humidity, received significant relief late Wednesday night as the weather took a dramatic turn. Heavy winds accompanied by rainfall began in several parts of Delhi. Similar conditions were reported in Greater Noida, where heavy rain was recorded, while rainfall also started in Sahibabad.
Strong gusts of wind swept through the region, and lightning was visible across the sky. The sudden change in weather led to a drop in temperatures, providing much-needed relief from the oppressive heat.
The weather department has issued an Orange Alert for Thursday, forecasting strong winds and rainfall, while a Yellow Alert has been announced for Friday. Pleasant weather conditions are expected to continue over the next two days, with temperatures likely to decrease further.
Delhi-NCR witnessed a sudden change in weather as rain lashed several parts of the region, bringing much-needed relief from the prevailing heat and humidity. The showers led to a noticeable drop in temperature, offering respite to residents who had been enduring uncomfortable weather conditions over the past few days.
Visuals from areas near the Indira Gandhi International (IGI) Airport on Sunday showed rain sweeping across several localities around the airport. The rainfall was accompanied by cloudy skies and cooler winds, improving outdoor conditions across the city.

The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast another spell of very light to light rainfall, accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds of up to 60 kmph, on June 11 and 12. According to the weather agency, these conditions may bring down the maximum temperature to around 35-37 degrees Celsius on June 12.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the southwest monsoon is likely to reach Delhi and the National Capital Region between June 25 and June 30. Noida, Gurugram and Ghaziabad are expected to receive the rains within the same window. The monsoon made landfall in Kerala on June 4, three days later than its usual June 1 arrival.
(With inputs from agencies)

articles.title: Heatwave news: Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan to face scorching heat till..., IMD data reveals all - news24online.com
articles.full_text:
Heatwave news: Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan to face scorching heat till…, IMD data reveals all
According to the latest weather bulletin issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the country is currently witnessing two extreme weather conditions at the same time. While severe heatwave conditions continue to grip the plains of northwest India, including Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, and Rajasthan, the monsoon has gained momentum across Kerala and several northeastern states. The weather department has also released an updated forecast detailing the duration of the heatwave, expected temperature changes, and rainfall activity across different regions. Here's a look at how long the scorching heat is likely to persist in your state and what the latest rain forecast says.
According to the latest weather bulletin issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the country is currently witnessing two extreme weather conditions at the same time. While severe heatwave conditions continue to grip the plains of northwest India, including Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, and Rajasthan, the monsoon has gained momentum across Kerala and several northeastern states. The weather department has also released an updated forecast detailing the duration of the heatwave, expected temperature changes, and rainfall activity across different regions. Here's a look at how long the scorching heat is likely to persist in your state and what the latest rain forecast says.
Heatwave to Continue in Delhi, UP and Rajasthan
According to the IMD, severe heatwave conditions are likely to continue across parts of Delhi, Haryana, Chandigarh, Punjab, western Uttar Pradesh, and western Rajasthan on June 10 and 11. Some areas of Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, and eastern Uttar Pradesh may also experience heatwave conditions.
Relief Expected from June 12
A fresh western disturbance is expected to become active over North India from the night of June 11. As a result, temperatures could drop by 4–6°C on June 12 and 13, bringing major relief from the intense heat across Delhi, UP, and other northern states.
Heavy Rain Alert in Kerala
The southwest monsoon is fully active over Kerala. The IMD has issued an Orange Alert for Alappuzha and Ernakulam districts, where very heavy rainfall of 11–20 cm is possible. Several other districts may witness moderate rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds of 40–50 kmph.
Storms and Humid Weather in Eastern India
Bihar and Gangetic West Bengal are likely to experience thunderstorms, lightning, and strong winds of 50–60 kmph. Heavy to very heavy rainfall is expected over Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Sikkim, coastal Karnataka, and sub-Himalayan West Bengal over the next few days. Odisha, Konkan, and Goa may continue to face hot and humid weather on June 10 and 11.
El Niño Risk Puts 197 Districts on Alert
The government has identified 197 districts that could be affected by a possible El Niño event this year. Agriculture authorities have prepared contingency plans, stocked seeds and farming inputs, and launched awareness campaigns to help farmers deal with any adverse impact on crops and rainfall.
Monsoon Likely to Remain Below Normal
The IMD has projected this year's southwest monsoon at around 90% of the Long Period Average (LPA), which falls in the "below normal" category. The monsoon has currently covered around 20–30% of the country and is expected to spread across the entire nation by July 15.

articles.full_text: दिल्ली-एनसीआर में मंगलवार रात तेज बारिश, गरज और धूल भरी आंधी ने मौसम का मिजाज पूरी तरह बदल दिया. इस दौरान हवाएं इतनी तेज चलीं कि पिछले कई दशकों के कुछ सबसे शक्तिशाली झोंके दर्ज किए गए. हालांकि, बारिश उतनी नहीं आई, जितनी उम्मीद की जा रही थी. देखें...

articles.title: आंधी-बारिश के साथ चलेंगी 60 KM प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से हवाएं...11 जून को दिल्ली में बदलेगा मौसम, IMD ने जारी किया ऑरेंज अलर्ट - NewsTak
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Delhi
articles.full_text:
आंधी-बारिश के साथ चलेंगी 60 KM प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से हवाएं...11 जून को दिल्ली में बदलेगा मौसम, IMD ने जारी किया ऑरेंज अलर्ट
Delhi Rain Alert: दिल्ली-NCR में भीषण गर्मी झेल रहे लोगों के लिए राहत की खबर है. मौसम विभाग ने 11 जून के लिए ऑरेंज अलर्ट जारी किया है. शाम होते-होते तेज आंधी, बारिश और गरज-चमक के आसार हैं, जिससे तापमान में गिरावट आएगी और अगले कुछ दिनों तक मौसम सुहाना रहने की उम्मीद है.
ADVERTISEMENT
Delhi Weather Today: दिल्ली-NCR के लोगों के लिए 11 जून राहत लेकर आ सकता है. पिछले कई दिनों से भीषण गर्मी और लू जैसे हालात झेल रही राजधानी में अब मौसम करवट लेने वाला है. मंगलवार शाम हुई बूदाबंदी के बाद तापमान में हल्की ठंडक महसूस की गई. इस बीच अब मौसम विभाग ने ताजा बुलेटिन जारी किया है. इसके अनुसार गुरुवार को भी दिल्ली NCR में दिनभर आंशिक से रूप से बादल छाए रहेंगे. वहीं शाम और रात के समय हल्की बारिश, गरज-चमक के साथ आंधी चलने की संभावना है. इसके साथ ही तेज हवाओं के चलने से लोगों को गर्मी से कुछ राहत मिल सकती है.
